footer.address: Ćirila i Metodija br. 96, 21000, Novi Sad, Serbia

hero.lead: Ice hockey at the heart of Vojvodina. Passion, teamwork, and fair play since 1957.

about.title: Passion on the ice, community off it
about.description: Founded in 1957, Vojvodina HC is a club that stands behind every player — whether a veteran or lacing up skates for the first time.

pillars.passionText: Every practice, every game — no compromises.
pillars.unityText: We play for each other, above all else.
pillars.fairText: We respect opponents, referees, and the game.
pillars.communityText: A club connecting generations of Vojvodina hockey.

cta.title: Ready for the ice?
cta.description: Join our practices — beginners welcome, gear can be rented on site.

newMembers.firstMonthDesc: New members skate for free during their entire first month — no commitment required.
newMembers.equipmentDesc: Full gear is available for all new members so you can try the ice without any upfront costs.

history.title: Club History
history.heroDescription: Nine-time national champions. Founded 1957. Always building.
history.structureDesc: The primary goals of HK Vojvodina members are the realisation of development plans and programmes, promotion of ice hockey, creating conditions for outstanding results, advancing pedagogical work in the sport, and promoting a culture of sportsmanship.
history.p1: Vojvodina Hockey Club from Novi Sad was founded in 1957. As a nine-time national champion, the club has always strived to demonstrate its leadership in the sport through a dedicated and organised approach.
history.p2: HKV is part of the Vojvodina Sports Society. We have been champions of SR Yugoslavia six times, once of Serbia and Montenegro (2003/04), and have won the SR Yugoslavia Cup three times. In 2009, we won the regional Pannonian League.
history.p3: The club suffered a devastating blow in March 2009 when a major fire at the SPENS ice arena destroyed the club premises and locker rooms, along with the senior team's complete equipment — which was competing in the playoff finals at the time. As a result, the club was inactive until 2015, when members of 'HK GentlemeNS', originally the veterans' section of Novi Sad's red and whites, revived the club. Since 2015, HKV has competed once again in the senior championship of Serbia.
history.p4: The club marked its 65th anniversary as the recipient of the November Charter, awarded by the City of Novi Sad for outstanding results and achievements — a recognition of decades of work in preserving the sporting spirit, teamwork, and competitive excellence. HK Vojvodina crowned that chapter by winning the Serbian senior championship title in the 2021/2022 season.
